title: Reading and Use of English - Part 6
description: You are going to read an article from which six sentences have been removed. Choose from the sentences A–G the one which fits each gap (37–42). There is one extra sentence which you do not need to use.

content.title: The Science of Sleep
content.text: We spend roughly a third of our lives doing it, yet sleep remains one of the most poorly understood states in biology. For much of human history, it was regarded simply as the absence of wakefulness — a passive shutdown. Modern neuroscience tells a very different story. (37) [37] Lejos de ser un estado pasivo, el cerebro realiza funciones vitales mientras dormimos. [/37] During sleep, the brain cycles through distinct stages of activity, each serving functions that wakefulness cannot replicate. || The most dramatic of these stages is Rapid Eye Movement sleep, or REM. It is during REM that most vivid dreaming occurs, and the brain's electrical activity closely resembles that of an alert, awake mind. (38) [38] En esta fase se consolidan los recuerdos y se procesan las emociones del día anterior. [/38] Research at the University of California has shown that students who sleep after studying retain significantly more information than those who study for the same period without sleeping. || Equally important is deep, non-REM sleep. During this phase, the brain appears to flush out toxic waste products — including the proteins associated with Alzheimer's disease — through a recently discovered system of fluid channels. (39) [39] Este proceso de limpieza nocturna podría tener consecuencias enormes para la salud cerebral a largo plazo. [/39] Scientists now believe that chronic sleep deprivation may be one of the factors contributing to the accumulation of these harmful proteins over time. || Sleep deprivation has a devastating impact on virtually every system in the body. Immune function, hormone regulation, cardiovascular health and metabolism all deteriorate significantly with insufficient sleep. (40) [40] Incluso una sola noche de sueño insuficiente tiene efectos medibles en el organismo. [/40] A study published in the journal Nature found that just one night of poor sleep reduced participants' immune cell activity by nearly a third. || The modern world is conspiring against sleep. Artificial light, particularly the blue wavelengths emitted by smartphone screens, suppresses the production of melatonin, the hormone that triggers sleepiness. (41) [41] El uso de pantallas antes de dormir retrasa el inicio del sueño y reduce su calidad. [/41] Employers and educators are beginning to take note: several schools in the United States have pushed back their start times following evidence that teenagers are biologically programmed to fall asleep later. || Changing the culture around sleep is perhaps the greatest challenge of all. (42) [42] En muchas sociedades, dormir poco se percibe erróneamente como una señal de productividad o esfuerzo. [/42] Sleep researchers argue that we need to reframe rest not as laziness but as the biological foundation of high performance — because the evidence overwhelmingly supports that view.

paragraphs.A: Far from being passive, the sleeping brain carries out complex and essential maintenance tasks.
paragraphs.B: This nocturnal cleansing process could have profound implications for long-term brain health.
paragraphs.C: Even a single night of poor sleep produces measurable changes in immune function and cognitive performance.
paragraphs.D: Screen use in the hours before bed delays sleep onset and reduces the proportion of restorative deep sleep.
paragraphs.E: Memories formed during the day are consolidated and emotional experiences are processed during this phase.
paragraphs.F: Some researchers argue that the quality of sleep matters far more than its total duration.
paragraphs.G: In many societies, sleeping less is mistakenly worn as a badge of productivity rather than recognised as a health risk.

questions.explanation: A corrige directamente la visión pasiva del sueño introducida antes, y abre la descripción de las funciones activas del cerebro dormido.
E detalla lo que ocurre en la fase REM (consolidación de memoria y procesamiento emocional), conectando con el estudio universitario siguiente.
B señala las implicaciones del proceso de limpieza cerebral nocturna, enlazando con la relación con el Alzheimer que sigue.
C concreta el impacto de una sola noche de mal sueño, preparando el estudio de Nature que lo cuantifica.
D especifica el efecto de las pantallas antes de dormir, conectando con las medidas que toman escuelas y empleadores.
F describe la percepción cultural errónea del sueño insuficiente como signo de productividad, que el artículo cierra rebatiendo.
